label: Web Game
description: For browser games and touch-first interactive experiences with asset and performance constraints.
positioning_text: Best when the product is an interactive browser experience that needs explicit device and performance planning.
question_set: What is the gameplay or interaction loop?
Which devices and browsers matter most?
Is the experience mobile-first or desktop-first?

specifications.title: Core Gameplay Loop and Controls
specifications.body: Define core gameplay loop, touch controls, fail/win states, and session length targets for players.

specifications.title: Rendering and Asset Pipeline
specifications.body: Document sprite/audio pipeline, compression strategy, cache policy, and runtime loading model.

specifications.title: Performance Budget and Device Matrix
specifications.body: Define FPS, memory, bundle-size budgets and target device/browser matrix with acceptance thresholds.

specifications.title: Progress Persistence and Resilience
specifications.body: Specify local/session persistence strategy, reconnect behavior, and graceful degradation for constrained networks.

specifications.title: Release and QA Deployment Pipeline
specifications.body: Define reproducible Docker Compose release pipeline, health checks, and browser QA access shape.

tasks.title: Prototype touch input and movement loop
tasks.description: Implement first playable loop with gesture and touch controls.

tasks.title: Build sprite atlas and lazy-loading asset manifest
tasks.description: Set up compressed textures and non-blocking asset loading.

tasks.title: Establish FPS and memory benchmarks for target devices
tasks.description: Create repeatable benchmark scenarios across baseline devices.

tasks.title: Implement save-state fallback and recovery flow
tasks.description: Persist progress with robust fallback for network interruptions.

tasks.title: Create Docker Compose deployment profile for browser QA
tasks.description: Expose the app on a predictable port for QA device testing.

rules.title: Gameplay frame budget is mandatory before feature sign-off
rules.body: Features are blocked from Completed if FPS and memory budgets fail on baseline devices.

rules.title: Asset payload growth must stay within agreed browser limits
rules.body: Bundle and texture growth requires explicit review when crossing performance thresholds.
